sentences.text: Howdy.
Hey, good morning, Jamie.
How are you?
Tired today.
Yeah.
Why?
Yeah, I don't blame you.
It's been a long week at the end of a long quarter, at the end of a long year.
It's been a grind, man.
Don't you feel that way?
Like, jeez.
Yeah.
It's been busy.
Oh,
all right, like every attempt to make your life easier has just like it got easier for a week and then you were back at the same place you were where it's just like constant.
Yeah, I mean the last two weeks have been a little bit quieter, but I, I, so many other like projects that he wants me to work on that it's like I now can finally focus on those,
um.
But You know, it is what it
is.
It is what it is, yeah, I hear you.
Um, I see Andy in the waiting room.
I'm the only person, or it
shouldn't be.
Uh, it's Andy and Michelle who are our two main points of contact.
They're like the leaders of the Cushman Fundraise team, and again this is Cushman folks, so they manage the fundraiser portfolio, um.
And it should largely be kind of on-site staff like our typical our typical user in in this group.
Cool.
And what is Andy's role?
What's their title?
Andy specifically, that's funny.
I, I always get them confused.
So Andy specifically.
is the regional marketing manager for client success.
And Michelle.
Michelle Hearn.
Do do do do do.
Michelle, where are you?
All right.
Michelle's the portfolio manager, so it's pretty, it's like Michelle portfolio manager and her regional is Andy, uh, and then everyone below her should be like slightly junior regionals and managers.
Beautiful.
All right, we'll go ahead and let them in.
Sounds good.
Let's rip it.
Good morning, Andy.
How are you?
Good morning.
I'm good.
How are you?
Good.
Happy Thursday.
Oh,
thank goodness.
We're almost there.
We can do that.
Um, OK, so wonderful.
Phil is kind of filled me in, uh, in regards to your team and what you're looking to kind of cover today.
Uh, do you anticipate any other folks joining us for today's training?
Not today.
Um, so, I saw your email, um, yesterday about Monica.
I'm gonna let Michelle make that decision.
She is talking back from a conference, so, um, I'm assuming she's gonna say yes, but, um, thanks for keeping us filled in.
Sure, absolutely.
Uh, OK, great.
So, sorry, um, I think I thought, and then this may have been a misunderstanding on my end.
I thought that we were gonna be training uh your regional managers and, and property managers today in this call.
I apologize, Andy, if I misunderstood.
No, it's OK.
Um.
Let me check.
So there's a couple on my calendar, so
yeah, I think I put one there and that was my mistake and then this was the correct one from Jamie's perspective, uh, because she runs these calls.
So all of our regionals are traveling back from a conference today, so if you want um to send this over, I can like the recording, I can send it over to them too.
And then I know we talked about getting everything implemented and then kind of looping in the on-site teams, um, if that works for you guys, but you tell me what works best.
I don't wanna waste anyone's time.
No, definitely, um, I think we are in a great spot.
It looks like we have all the properties moved over.
Uh, from Fundri, so we are in a good spot in that place.
If we want to maybe use this call, Jamie can walk you through one of those properties.
Uh, we could just get really comfortable with the functionality and then send this email as an email to the rest of the group that they have that resource.
Yeah, that'd be great.
Awesome.
All right.
Well, let's go ahead and get started then.
So Andy, have we logged into apartment IQ yet?
No.
OK.
Do you have a login?
I do not.
OK, well, let's start there.
Let's make sure we get you logged in and set up.
So, uh, first and foremost, um, are you aware of what apartment IQ is and what it does?
Yeah, the basics.
OK, perfect.
So for today, we'll just focus on the uh foundations, but just to kind of formally define it for you and anybody else who watches this call.
Apartment IQ is an automated market survey tool.

And so what we do is we look to landlord websites on a nightly basis and pull in that information for your property as well as your comps so that way your teams don't have to manually input data, call around.
We're going to do all that heavy lifting for you so that way you can log in, see the data that you need, extract it in reports, and get on with your day.
Uh, we're currently tracking upwards of 3 million properties in the United States, and it's really easy to request additional ones, and I'll show you how to do that in just a bit.
All right, so to get us started, let's go ahead and just take a peek at the users here, and we can get you added in.
Uh, so, are you working on that right now or I'm happy to get her added too?
Yeah,
I'm doing that right now because I want to make sure I also tie Andy's access to the specific fundrise.
Portfolio.
So I'm just looking for that list of properties, but I'll take care of that now while you go over this area.
Beautiful.
OK, perfect.
Well, Andy, let's go ahead and jump into one of the owned and managed properties.
Um, which property would you like to take a peek at today?
Um, let's look at Highland townhomes.
Let's see, here we go.
All right, perfect.
So apartment IQ is going to be broken up into several different reports.

This first one here is going to be really similar to perhaps what your teams would have collected, um, and we're gonna showcase things like advertise occupancy, exposure, least percentage, concessions, pricing, fees, deposits, and amenities, and this is also where we can actually set up automated reports that we can get delivered to our email inbox.
Now there's a lot of other reports that I'll briefly show you today, but unit types will break down information regarding your threes, your fours, or whatever present at your property.
If you're overseeing properties that have multiple floor plans, you'll see those here as well.
And then we can also access history regarding a market and your property.
Uh, now it looks like we have just about 6 months of data on your property, but for some of the comps in your area, we could have upwards of 4 years of data.
So I'll showcase that to you in just a minute here.
And then the unit's report will show you every unit or um uh yeah, every unit that has either leased or become available in the last 30 days.
We'll talk about that over here in the unit's report.
All right, so the first thing I wanna talk about here is are the comps that you're seeing.
Are we looking in terms of the comps?
Is there anything that's missing, anything that uh you would consider to not be a cop?
Um
Nope, those all look.
Yeah, those all look good.
This one's all I, well, I feel like all of our sites are tough because they're all SFR town homes,
um, the Charleston market, the way the zip codes are lined out is, is tricky, but those all look good.
OK, perfect.
Um, now we do have different levels of permissioning with an apartment IQ, and my guess is you'll probably have permissions to change these comps at any time if you would like to.
You'll find that button in the top right hand corner, and it will provide you some insight into other possibilities if you wanted to explore other options.

So we will provide you with information in regards to where your property currently is positioned and then all of the possibilities out there if you wanted to do some exploring, uh, it's very easy to just simply hover over one of these dots, click add comp, or even search for it, uh, in a list of similar properties that will provide to you.
Now we're going to base it off of a couple different things.
All of the comp scores and the suggestions that we give you are essentially based on those metrics that you're seeing on the screen.
So distance, you're built or renovated, the total unit count, price points, and We're also going to include story count.
So we know that for example with the townhome, you're probably not gonna want to compare yourself to a high rise.
So we're going to take all of that into account when we're calculating these scores here that you're seeing and also based off of the units that we're suggesting as similar to you.
So really easy to change and manipulate, can remove with ease, add with ease, and even search by name if you need as well.
Just taking a look at this list, is there anything that um you're noticing on here that perhaps you would want to include or things that you're surprised at?
Um,
No, not really.
Um.
I guess the Burke may be the most surprising it is comparable like in location, but um it's more multi-family.
Well, a lot of these are multi-family, but um.
No, like I said, the SFR is just when you think like multi-family, it's very black and white.
SFR is not.
Yeah,
yeah, that totally makes sense.
Um, OK, well, in terms of the metrics that you're gonna be able to see here, by default, these are the ones that we're gonna showcase to you.
The great news is you get to customize this in any way that you'd like.
You just simply click customize metrics and include or exclude any metric within any of these fields based on your needs.
So if you want to see average rent or net effective rent across the set, you can definitely see that.
In addition, you can include things like total availability and vacancy, exposure, all the concessions, anything that's important to you, and then hit apply.
And all of that data will start pulling in for you.
Now, unlike some other tools out there where it's kind of preset and you can only sort by a couple of different metrics, we allow you to sort by literally anything.
So if you wanted to sort by applications, by least percentage, it's actually gonna move it for you within the set to show you where you're positioned in the market.
In addition to that, if you have a really big comp set here, you can always pin it to the left and then scroll and compare, but this is a really healthy one.
We typically see somewhere between 5 to 15 comps, so this is a great A great set here.
Now in terms of some of the metrics here, if you ever have questions, just hover over the metric and it'll give you some more information.

So for example, with the applications here, we're showing you how many units have actually been applied to uh at your property or at your comps, and you can actually click on it to see those actual um units if you will.

So here you can see all the rent pricing that was charged for those, the days on market and even be able to view the history regarding those units, uh, to see what was it listed at when it was initially published, you might see a gap if it.

Taken offline or hidden with filters, uh, but we will, if it lives within like I think a 90 day window, we will still consider it part of the same listing here and so you'll see that um on the 14th when it was applied to what the unit was actually listed for.
And then relisted is gonna tell you units that go back online.
So if somebody didn't qualify or they just uh backed out of the deal, you'll see those relisted.
If a unit stays offline for more than 7 days, we're gonna count it as an official lease here for you.
Do you have any questions about any of the other metrics that you're seeing on the screen?
No, this is amazing.
Awesome.
Now, at any time you can also click on a property up here to get what we would refer to as a rent roll for that property.
We'll show you everything that has leased or has become available.
We'll show you the most recent lease for that property.
Uh, and then if it has multiple, uh, uh, floor plans or bed types here, we'll show you that full unit mix.
So it looks like this property just has 3 beds, so you'll see that unit mix over here.
Again, this is slightly different than um like a full unit mix because we can only showcase to you what we've detected as available over time.
So just wanna make sure that that's clear.
You can always sort this to see if there's any units that are missing.
Uh, it typically encouraged that because then you can see are their units missing, perhaps that they're using as models or that are employee housing or things like that.
All right, so we're also gonna show you everything regarding pricing.
By default, it's gonna be set to absolute minimum, but you can toggle this to average as well.
So just depends on what you need that day, um, we typically find that people kind of move between both of those uh filters here and everything here again is sortable.
So if I want to sort by rent or an effective rent, very easy to sort that to see where I'm positioned in the market.
And then we're gonna show you everything regarding fees, deposits, and amenities down here.

So it makes it really easy for folks on your teams to see uh the fees running in the market, what amenities your property provides over a direct competitor makes it very easy to prove your value add proposition for your property and your uh town home units over a direct competitor.

All right, now, if at any time you have questions about the data that you're seeing or want to submit an update for something, maybe for example, we got the year built wrong or uh the total unit count is off, we're able to override that information and we can make sure we get it right.
So in the case that you need to update anything, you just literally let us know here.
So if we were updating perhaps the year built for the Highland.

All you would do is choose the name of the property, choose the metric, and then let us know what it is, so maybe it was 2024, and then you go how to hit submit, and then our team will make the adjustments for you.
Perfect.
All right.
And then we've got two different report styles that you can extract.
So a lot of folks that are like in property management will really leverage this PDF report frequently.
This one is more of like an executive style report, um, and it will include 7 day trends for the market.
And so a lot of folks will have their teams.

Set this up to deliver perhaps on days that you have pricing calls or one on ones with your uh property managers or leasing teams, so that way they have that data right at their fingertips and again, not having to call around, not having to secret shop or, you know, browse their websites.
We're gonna pull it all in for you so that way they have that report um on that given day.
So we'll give us just a second to download.
I've been noticing that my internet has been a little bit slow, so I apologize for having to close and open that a million times.
No, you're fine.
All right, so here we go.
So it's going to be created for your market specifically, and it's gonna show trends in the last 7 days for that market, for your bed types.
So this is a better one to show the 3 beds.
If there's nothing listed, it simply means that they don't have anything available for that bed type, or maybe they just don't have that bed type at all.
And then the concessions page is my favorite because it makes it really easy to see what is running in the market and if there are any restrictions.
And then the last report type that we have is this Excel version.
This one is based on anything you've customized, so any metrics you've included or excluded, filters you've applied.
Uh, this report will give you a very robust look into the market in general.
It's gonna provide us with 5 different um Excel versions of these uh reports in here.
So it's gonna give us a property summary, which will show us every uh thing regarding our total comp set over the last 30, 60, and 90 days.
So all the performance metrics, total listings, the pricing.
We'll also give you that market survey, so gone are the days of having to manual manually create this.
We've also got bed count data, floor plan data for you, and a report of every active listing we've ever seen since it's been tracked in apartment IQ we can date back to October of 2020.
Wow.
Yeah, lots of data here.
Just out of curiosity, which report do you think you'll find more useful like in your day to day?
Um, probably the PDF.
OK.
Do you think that you'll have your teams perhaps schedule those, uh, to send to themselves as well?
Yeah, um, and I'm not sure how Funrise will want it to.
I'm assuming they'll want the PDF too, but it could be wrong, um, but I think that will be the most useful for them in day to day and on our, our weekly calls.
Perfect.
All right, so just to kind of get a pulse check, what are your thoughts?
Are you I'm like, where was this?

I mean, obviously I think like, you know, the old fashioned market survey is just like, you know, your good bone work, but everyone should know how to do that, but this is when you're talking 40 plus sites, this is amazing.
We hope that it will save you and your teams a ton of time.
Oh my gosh, yeah, and just the consistency and accurate information is really helpful.
Yes, yes, gone are the days of having folks plug in the information from last week.
Oh my gosh.
Awesome.
OK, well, I, I'm gonna showcase just a couple of these at a high level.
These ones could be helpful if you needed to dig into your bed type performance.
We're gonna show you everything regarding key pricing metrics that you would expect, uh, price per square foot metrics, all those uh good data points here.
Uh, but if you need to dig in a little deeper, that floor plan report can be very helpful as well.
So, a very common use case here is people like to look specifically at their property.
So perhaps you just want to see performance across the last week or even in the last year or 2 or 3 years as well.
You can dig into history here to just kind of see performance over time.
So if I wanted to view the last 3 months of data, I'd be able to see how many units we've ever seen available for a particular floor plan.
All of the units that we've seen leased during that time frame.

The average rent during that time frame availability, so all the units that were available, you'll see the ones marked that are currently available and or the ones that have been leased since that time.
And then we'll also show you any of the rent pricing and trends.
So it looks like our um prices have gone up just a little bit in the last 3 months, and you can see that reflected here.
And then folks really like to be able to just see what are your comps priced at, right?

So being able to compare apples to apples, so maybe Highland to uh this particular property or Highland to all properties, uh just allows you to see the average square footage to be able to compare um perhaps units or floor plans that are comparable in size to kind of see how are they priced versus how are you priced.
Then if you need to see the market as a whole, to view the history there, this historical report can be very helpful.
Again, we can access up to 4 years of history here.
We can break it down by property or by bed type.

So for example, if you know, you're having a really hard time moving your 4 bedroom town homes and you want to see like what's happening in the market, uh historically, you can click that or 3 beds to be able to view the data over time.

By default it's gonna show you in the last year, week over week, and then you can toggle between whatever metrics makes sense uh based on your needs that day, but a lot of people will come over here just to cross compare their properties' performance to a direct competitor.
Do you have like a property that you would consider like Uh, directly in line in terms of a competitor here in the market.
Um,
They're so hard.
Sumner Village would probably be, it's another Funrise site, um, but it's like less than 1 mile, um, they're new builds, but they're all
like SFR detached, so.
Staying.
Let me see here.
I wonder if they have started.
Don't see any data here.
It's interesting, cause we're capturing leasing data.
So, do you wanna perhaps send in a ticket to support?
Do you know why we wouldn't be seeing any data here for pricing?
Yeah, that's really odd.
Uh, the only thing I can think of is that perhaps there was a change in the website, but why would we have availability and no rents?
Yeah, that's interesting.
So we should be showcasing the um the pricing here, but for some reason we're not capturing the rent pricing

is odd because we are able to see, for example, the availability, so we'll have our product team look at why it's not showcasing here, but um, yeah, it just allows you to just directly compare the pricing over time and be able to dig into more recent history or further into the the past.
Uh, being able to see what was available week over week, month over month, uh, as a direct competitor, and just to be able to see your total availability versus theirs.
The leasing activity, so who had the most leases week over week, that just allows you just direct visibility into that and a very uh easy to read a report.
And then last but not least is the exposure.
So when you lease upset the market, you're obviously they're gonna be pretty exposed.
You can watch how they stabilize over time and the impact that that has on the market too.
Oh, that's amazing.
Yeah.
What kind of key, uh, like initiatives or um Uh, business problems, do you think that this report could help you solve?
Oh my gosh, so many,
um, I think just the day to day, um, obviously when the teams come to us and, you know, leasing has been slow.
My first suggestion and they want to go to Funrise with the price decrease or concession.
I'm like, have you completed a market survey yet?
Um, just so we know what's in the market.
And so it's just that I think that day to day is just gonna make things so much easier for them.
It's also gonna let them know like, hey, if we've been slow this week, have, you know, our comps been slow too, or no, they've had 10 leases, well, why?

Um, and so I think it's just gonna cut down on that and I mean, this just shows really everything that I mean, especially on our weekly calls with Fun eyes, this shows everything that they're gonna ask.
So this is just, I cannot explain to you how excited we are about it.
Yay!
Well, that's exactly what we hope is that this will be really helpful for you so that you can leverage this and save you a ton of time from having to collect that manually.
All right, the last report that I want to show you in here is the units report.
Now, a lot of folks directly on site love this report because it allows them visibility into individualized units um in their total market.
Now, leveraging the filters is really the key, because otherwise it's just a list of a ton of data.

So typically what we would suggest is comparing properties perhaps that a potential renter is considering, properties that you're trying to dig into more for why have we been, you know, struggling with selling two beds?
Has it been the same problem for X property down the street?
You'll be able to kind of dig into that a little bit more explicitly within this report.
So for example, with Highland, maybe you wanted to look at the uh differences between the Sumner Village and Highland.
We can apply a filter here to see that.
So let me just make sure, yeah, we are pulling in the units.
Now we can from there zoom in a little bit closer.

So maybe you just want to look at the 4 beds in this market, the 3 beds in this market, can also do it by size, rent price, date available, unit styles, if you need to filter by a little bit more, you can do that here.
And let me just see what we got here, a mixture of threes and fours.
So let's filter just by threes for this example.
Um, and from there down at the bottom, we can then sort and filter by available or at least.

So if you wanted to see what least in the last 30 days, makes it very easy to see that, or if your teams want to know what's available and how are we priced in comparison, you can toggle this on and then sort by any metric that makes sense.
Commonly people will sort by rent or net effective rent, just so that they can understand where their units are kind of sitting in the market in comparison to a competitor.
Provides you insights into the individualized unit, bed bath configuration, all of the rent pricing and concessions price, the price per square foot.
For some units, we'll even show you the best lease length if we can find it.
And then all of the price history and rent changes as well.

So one of my favorite things is when I can find a potential unit that maybe is a little cheaper than a potential competitor, which that's interesting why we're not pulling in square footage metrics here, that might be related to that bug with the pricing.
Yeah, something bizarre is going on with Sumner Village.
Our team's checking it out now.
Yeah, we're gonna dig into it for you, but I love it when I can find a unit, perhaps that is a little cheaper, and perhaps the square footage is a little bit larger.
It's a great way to really entice the runners to rent from you and your teams will love it too just because it really provides them and arms them with the data uh to really entice those runners.
OK.
So to just recap, uh, the most important points within the market survey, make sure that those comps are right.

If there's ever any comps that need to be adjusted in any of your markets, please feel free to go ahead and make those adjustments, would encourage you and your teams to customize the metrics, all user base, so how you set your metrics can be different than how your property managers or your leasing specialists set their metrics.
All of this is unique to you.
I would encourage you to get your teams to set up those automated PDFs, so that way it gets delivered right to them.
And then the last thing that I wanted to point out is our help center.
So we have a ton of resources in here from getting started guides to frequently asked questions and answers.

We also have a glossary, so if you need to provide direct calculations to the fundraise team or to any other key stakeholders, this can be really helpful uh to do that, and I'll drop it in the chat for you so you can check it out, but there's a lot of resources in there, so I encourage you to take a peek there.
Perfect.
I do have one last section that I wanted to show you, which is our explore function, but do you have any other questions regarding the market survey?
I don't think so, not right now.
Now, over in the explore function, this is gonna allow you to explore markets and submarkets.
Um, talk to me about like uh how you currently review um or do you currently review markets or submarkets in your area to just kind of see what else is out there.
Um,
But it's probably not the most efficient way, but usually just by like Google Maps, zip code, and typing in zip codes in the Zillow, to be honest.
Yeah.
Well,
I've got great news for you because the Explorer function hopefully will completely get rid of that for you.
So we're going to essentially do the same thing.
We're using Google Maps to source this information, but we're gonna provide you with direct visibility into the key things that you're looking for, right?
Like the pricing, the all of those key metrics that you just want to hover over and and see without having to click in.
So can you remind me where that property was that we were just looking at?
Uh North Charleston.
OK, so if we were to click Charleston specifically, what it will do is provide you with insight into that market.
Now, right now we're showing you 793 properties, but the great thing is when you zoom in or zoom out, we're gonna provide you with more and more data uh dots if you will.
In addition to that, you can also click on different submarkets.
So if we got the market wrong, you can click on like North Charleston, or this particular area of Mount Pleasant.
So you can zoom in on those different areas and very easily hover over the various properties to see what are they charging.
View more details if you want, by going into their website.
This is gonna give you a generalized snapshot.
Of the market's performance.
My favorite thing is that you can actually filter it down by whatever makes sense.
So if you're just trying to compare properties that are similar to yours, we can very easily sort by your built, total unit count.

We can also sort by construction type, which would be very helpful for town homes, being able to look specifically at 1 to 3 story uh units, as well as specific amenities that you want to cross compare to.
So this report I think will be really, really helpful for you rather than using Google
Maps.
Yes, I love this.
Awesome.
All right, we'll definitely encourage you to check this section out too, and, you know, loop fill in on any feedback or suggestions you have here.

We're always looking to make this better and more functional, uh, so please let us know if there's any additional filters that you'd like to see or additional context, uh, that you'd like to see here, but we're really excited that you're, you're here and leveraging apartment IQ and if you have any questions or need additional support, we're here to support you and your team.
Awesome.
Thank you guys so much.
I appreciate you.
Absolutely, no problem, Andy.
I'll go ahead and follow up uh when this recording spins up so that you have that as reference and our team is in the middle of taking a look at that survey.
So just so we have some clarity, I'll follow up with you there as well so we can feel really confident about what's going on.
All right.
Awesome.
No worries, I appreciate it.
Right.
All righty.
Well, hope you have a great rest of your day and a great weekend.
You too, guys.
All right, see you.
